event1.text: You saw the Incident Alpha report.

event6.text: Those records are incomplete. Not damaged. Incomplete.

event8.text: Most archive sectors were corrupted during the blackout, but corruption doesn't usually choose what to erase.

event13.text: Or at least they should be.

event1.text: That is not the answer you want. It is also, unfortunately, the only honest one I have.

event2.text: If I find out I buried something on purpose, you will hear it from me first.

event0.text: Thank you for not turning this into an interrogation yet.

event1.text: Give it ten minutes. I may deserve one.

event1.title: ARCHIVE MAP
event1.content:
Recovered maintenance map of the station archive sector.

Sector A: Primary relay archive
Sector B: Corrupted personnel cache
Sector C: Emergency channel records
Sector D: Quarantined backup channel index

event3.text: Sector C. Emergency channel records. That shouldn't still be indexed.

event4.text: Sector D definitely should not be indexed.

event1.text: There may still be something left inside. If the archive survived, answers survived.

event2.text: That's the comforting version. The less comforting version is that someone wanted it to survive.

event4.title: ENCRYPTED STORAGE DEVICE
event4.content:
[ENCRYPTED ARCHIVE FRAGMENT]

Archive Key Hint: ALPHA

Warning: Failed decryption attempts may trigger quarantine response.

Status: Locked

event3.text: Careful. The archive noticed the wrong key.

event4.text: Try the hint exactly. No guessing. I am very tired of systems punishing curiosity.

event1.title: RECORDING TRANSCRIPT
event1.content:
21:14 Door 12 opened.
21:15 Unidentified movement detected.
21:17 Security override accepted.
21:19 Signal interruption.
21:20 Casualty report created.
21:22 Archive deletion command issued.

event6.text: That recording should not exist. And before you ask, yes, I know how terrible that sounds.

event1.text: Because I deleted that archive myself.

event3.text: I know. That is exactly the kind of sentence that makes people stop trusting you.

event1.text: Some records were never meant to survive.

event2.text: And I am beginning to think this one survived on purpose.

event1.title: CORRIDOR CAMERA FRAME
event1.caption: Recovered frame from Door 12 archive feed. Timestamp damaged.

event3.text: That figure... the angle is wrong, but the suit outline is familiar.

event4.text: I hate when evidence has better memory than I do.

event1.title: SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG
event1.content:
Override User: REDACTED
Authorization: VALID
Source: UNKNOWN
Command Chain: Door 12 / Archive Delete / Emergency Channel Wake
event1.message: [SECURITY OVERRIDE LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: Authorization valid. Source unknown. That's not how security logs work.

event4.text: Unless the system accepted something that looked like us.

event0.title: EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE
event0.content:
Emergency Backup Channel

7.14 MHz

Use only if primary communication systems fail.

Warning: Do not open if unauthorized return signal is detected.
event0.message: [EMERGENCY CHANNEL NOTE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: No, no, no. We are not touching that channel.

event2.text: Whatever happens next... Do not open channel 7.14.

event3.text: I don't care what it promises you. I don't care if it sounds like me.

event2.text: And if it answers... don't answer back.
Then don't make the same mistake we did.

event3.text: We thought backup channels were safer. Turns out safety can be very loud when it's lying.
THANK YOU FOR OPENING THE DOOR

event4.text: No. No, close it. Close it now.
